name: W7 Push Morning Flow
description: 15 min. Beefed up shoulder health block — more bodyweight shoulder strength/rehab. No equipment needed. Overhead mobility + shoulder stability before push press.

exercises.notes: Lie on your side, elbow at 90, rotate forearm up. No band needed — gravity is the resistance. Targets the rotator cuff. Go slow.
Face down, lift arms in a Y shape overhead. Hits lower traps + rotator cuff. Hold at the top for 1 second.
Push-up position, arms locked. Only the shoulder blades move — sink down (retract) then push the floor away (protract). Strengthens serratus anterior.
Back flat against wall. Getting deeper each week.
Open up the chest and hip flexors.

tips: 15 min — shoulder health block is bigger this week. All bodyweight, no equipment.
Side-lying external rotation: the simplest rotator cuff exercise. Slow is the point.
Prone Y raises: if your lower traps are weak, these will burn fast. That's good.
Scapular push-ups: serratus anterior stabilizes your shoulder during all pressing. Train it.
If short on time: shoulder health block only. 5 min of the most important stuff.
